reasoning: User asked for symptoms of prostate cancer. Provide a concise, 1–3 sentence answer focused on common symptoms and note that early disease can be asymptomatic; keep plain language without medical instructions.
output: Early prostate cancer usually causes no symptoms. If symptoms occur, they can include more frequent urination (especially at night), difficulty starting or stopping urination, weak or interrupted urine flow, painful or burning urination, blood in urine or semen, painful ejaculation, erectile problems, and pain or stiffness in the lower back, hips, or upper thighs; these symptoms can also be due to non-cancer conditions like an enlarged prostate.
